description: A domestic interior scene depicting the first observation of the transit of Venus. At left, a man in 17th-century scholarly attire stands at a desk or table near a window, through which a dramatic beam of light enters the room, projecting onto a surface. At right, a woman seated in a chair holds a small child, with another child nearby, all illuminated by the shaft of sunlight. The contrast between the dark interior and the brilliant beam of light crossing the room creates a striking chiaroscuro effect. Books, papers, and instruments are scattered about, suggesting a scholar's study.

_meta.title: First observation of the transit of Venus
